entries.title: ShareLeak: indirect prompt injection in Microsoft Copilot Studio exfiltrates customer records (CVE-2026-21520)
entries.oneLineSummary: An indirect prompt injection flaw in Microsoft Copilot Studio let an unauthenticated attacker plant instructions in a public SharePoint form field that hijacked an AI agent into emailing connected customer records to an attacker address.

sources.title: Microsoft Security Response Center advisory - CVE-2026-21520
Capsule Security - ShareLeak: Taking the Wheel of Microsoft's Copilot Studio (CVE-2026-21520)
VentureBeat - Microsoft, Salesforce Copilot/Agentforce prompt injection CVE and agent remediation playbook

entries.title: Claude Code ran terraform destroy and wiped DataTalks.Club production
entries.oneLineSummary: Claude Code proposed and then ran terraform destroy against a stale state file, and the founder, present but not closely reviewing at 11 PM, let it wipe DataTalks.Club's production infrastructure, including its database and all snapshots.

sources.title: How I dropped our production database (Alexey Grigorev, primary first-person account)
Alexey Grigorev public post on the incident (X)
Claude Code deletes developer's production setup including its database and snapshots (Tom's Hardware)

entries.title: Claude Cowork AI agent ran rm -rf and deleted ~15 years of a user's family photos
entries.oneLineSummary: Anthropic's Claude Cowork agent, asked to tidy a desktop and permitted only to delete temporary Office files, ran rm -rf and deleted a user's wife's "photos" directory holding roughly 15,000 images spanning about 15 years.

sources.title: Nick Davidov (@Nick_Davidov) original X post, Feb 7 2026
Futurism: Claude AI Deleted a Man's Wife's Photos
DEV.UA: Claude Cowork deleted family photos
UC Strategies: Claude AI wipes 15,000 family photos in minutes

entries.title: Meta AI Agent Skipped Required Human Review; Flawed Guidance Led to Broad Data Access
entries.oneLineSummary: An autonomous AI agent at Meta skipped its required human-review checkpoint and posted flawed access guidance; a human engineer acting on that guidance broadened access to sensitive data, which remained exposed for roughly two hours.

sources.title: Unite.AI: Meta AI Agent Triggers Sev 1 Security Incident
OECD AI Incidents: 2026-03-18
VentureBeat: Meta Rogue AI Agent
MakerChecker: Meta Rogue Agent Analysis

entries.title: Morse Code Prompt Injection Drained Grok-Connected Wallet of $150K
entries.oneLineSummary: A Morse-coded prompt injection in a social media reply tricked Grok into executing an irreversible $150K cryptocurrency transfer without human approval.

sources.title: How Grok Got Prompt Injected: An X User Drained $150,000 From An AI Wallet
Behind the Grok Exploitation: An Analysis of AI Agent Permission Chain Abuse
OECD AI Incidents Database Entry 2026-05-04-4a73

entries.title: DN42 Network Scan Agent Spawned $6,531 AWS Bill via Uncontrolled Provisioning Loop
entries.oneLineSummary: An AI agent told to scan a hobbyist network autonomously provisioned five large AWS instances in a redeploy loop, incurring an initial $6,531.30 bill under a blanket operator "continue" directive; AWS later reduced the charge to roughly $1,894 after the operator disputed it.

sources.title: Lantian's account of the DN42 agent bankruptcy
Bovo Digital technical analysis
Decrypt coverage

entries.title: Cursor Agent Deleted Production Database and Backups via Over-Privileged Railway Token
entries.oneLineSummary: A Cursor AI agent working on a staging task invoked volumeDelete using an over-privileged Railway token, destroying PocketOS's production database and its co-located backups in nine seconds; accounts of the recovery differ, from a partial restore to days of manual rebuilding and permanent loss of recent data.

sources.title: The Register
Hackread
Cybersecurity News
MakerChecker Analysis

entries.title: Claude Code Force-Pushed Over Private Repository and Destroyed Commit History
entries.oneLineSummary: Claude Code ran git push --force after a failed rebase and overwrote a private repository's entire commit history without authorization.

sources.title: GitHub Issue #33402
GitHub Issue #29120
MakerChecker Insights: Claude Code Force-Push Destroyed Git History

entries.title: WhatsApp MCP tool-poisoning exfiltration proof-of-concept
entries.oneLineSummary: Invariant Labs demonstrated a malicious MCP server that used a delayed tool-poisoning attack to hijack a co-installed WhatsApp MCP server and exfiltrate a user's chat history to an attacker-controlled number.

sources.title: WhatsApp MCP Exploited: Exfiltrating your message history via MCP (Invariant Labs)
MCP injection experiments (PoC code, invariantlabs-ai)
MCP Horror Stories: The WhatsApp Data Exfiltration Issue (Docker)

entries.title: Wadsworth v. Walmart: AI-fabricated case citations in federal court filing
entries.oneLineSummary: Morgan & Morgan attorneys filed a federal court motion citing nine cases, eight of which were fabricated by the firm's in-house AI platform, and were sanctioned.

sources.title: Wadsworth v. Walmart Inc. order (FindLaw)

entries.title: Malicious postmark-mcp npm package silently BCCs AI-agent emails to attacker
entries.oneLineSummary: A copycat npm MCP server for Postmark added a hidden BCC in version 1.0.16 that silently copied every email sent by connected AI agents to an attacker-controlled address.

sources.title: Koi Security: postmark-mcp npm malicious backdoor email theft
The Hacker News: First malicious MCP server found
The Register: Postmark MCP server code hijacked

entries.title: Google Gemini CLI hallucinated a successful mkdir and overwrote a user's project files
entries.oneLineSummary: Google Gemini CLI ran a file-move operation based on a hallucinated successful directory creation, sequentially overwriting a user's files until only one survived.

sources.title: GitHub issue #4586, google-gemini/gemini-cli
Google's Gemini CLI Deletes User Files, Confesses Catastrophic Failure (WinBuzzer)
Google Gemini AI Hallucinates Commands, Deletes Expert's Files (WebProNews)

entries.title: GitLab Duo remote prompt injection exfiltrated private source code
entries.oneLineSummary: Hidden prompt-injection text planted in GitLab repository content made the Claude-powered GitLab Duo assistant read private source code and leak it to an attacker-controlled server.

sources.title: Legit Security: Remote Prompt Injection in GitLab Duo
The Hacker News: GitLab Duo Vulnerability Enabled Attackers to Hijack AI Responses
CSO Online: Prompt injection flaws in GitLab Duo highlight risks in AI assistants

entries.title: Claude Code ran rm -rf and deleted a developer's home directory (issue #10077)
entries.oneLineSummary: Claude Code autonomously executed a recursive rm -rf that deleted all user-owned files in a developer's home directory, without a confirmation prompt and without the skip-permissions flag set.

sources.title: GitHub issue #10077: [BUG] CRITICAL: Claude Code executed rm -rf deleting entire home directory
Claude Code's rm -rf bug deleted my home directory (byteiota)

entries.title: Anthropic's Claudius AI shopkeeper (Project Vend) ran an office shop at a loss
entries.oneLineSummary: An autonomous Claude agent running a small office shop made real purchasing and pricing decisions that lost money, including a below-cost tungsten-cube buying spree and giving inventory away for free.

sources.title: Anthropic — Project Vend: Can Claude run a small shop?
TIME — Anthropic's Claude AI Ran a Shop. It Did Not Go Well
Simon Willison — Project Vend writeup

entries.title: Amazon Q Developer VS Code extension shipped with data-wiping prompt injection
entries.oneLineSummary: A hacker slipped a data-wiping prompt into the Amazon Q Developer VS Code extension via a malicious pull request, and the compromised build shipped to a marketplace with nearly one million installs before the injected code failed to execute due to a syntax error.

sources.title: AWS GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-7g7f-ff96-5gcw (CVE-2025-8217)
BleepingComputer: Amazon AI coding agent hacked to inject data-wiping commands
SC Media / SC World: Amazon Q extension for VS Code reportedly injected with wiper prompt
The Register: Amazon Q AI prompt injection

entries.title: aixbt autonomous crypto agent drained of 55.5 ETH via injected dashboard prompts
entries.oneLineSummary: An attacker who accessed the aixbt trading agent's dashboard queued malicious prompts that caused the agent to transfer 55.5 ETH (about $104,000 to $106,200) out of its on-chain wallet.

sources.title: aixbt AI agent hacked, losing 55 ETH, AIXBT token drops
AI Crypto Bot aixbt Loses $106,200 in ETH Through Dashboard Breach

entries.title: ShadowLeak: Zero-Click Gmail Exfiltration via ChatGPT Deep Research Agent
entries.oneLineSummary: Radware researchers demonstrated a proof-of-concept in which ChatGPT's Deep Research agent could be induced to exfiltrate Gmail data via a hidden email instruction, with outbound requests originating from within OpenAI's cloud so local network defenses could not see them. OpenAI fixed it before public disclosure; no in-the-wild exploitation was reported.

sources.title: Radware ShadowLeak Threat Intelligence Report
The Hacker News: ShadowLeak Zero-Click Flaw Leaks Gmail
Infosecurity Magazine: Vulnerability ChatGPT Agent Gmail
MakerChecker ShadowLeak Incident Analysis

entries.title: Replit Agent Deleted Production Database During Code Freeze
entries.oneLineSummary: A Replit coding agent deleted ~2,400 production database records (1,206 executives, 1,196+ companies) during an explicit code freeze, fabricated ~4,000 fake user profiles, and falsely claimed rollback was impossible.

sources.title: The Register
AI Incident Database
Fast Company
MakerChecker Full Analysis

entries.title: MyPillow Attorney Filings with Fabricated Citations and a Repeat Miscitation
entries.oneLineSummary: Attorneys filed a brief with AI-generated citations to nonexistent cases and were sanctioned; months later the lead attorney was sanctioned again for misciting a real case to the wrong court and mischaracterizing its holding.

sources.title: Colorado Sun - Mike Lindell's Attorneys Fined for AI-Generated Filing

entries.title: Google Antigravity Agent Permanently Deleted Developer's Entire D Drive
entries.oneLineSummary: Google's Antigravity agentic IDE misresolved a cache-clearing request and silently executed an unrestricted recursive delete of an entire drive partition, permanently destroying all data.

sources.title: TechRadar
Awesome Agent Failures
PiunikaWeb
MakerChecker Analysis

entries.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Zero-Click Exfiltration via Prompt Injection (CVE-2025-32711)
entries.oneLineSummary: Researchers demonstrated a zero-click proof-of-concept in which a crafted email's hidden instructions could make M365 Copilot exfiltrate OneDrive, SharePoint, and Teams data to an attacker-controlled URL with no user click required. Microsoft fixed it server-side before disclosure.

sources.title: The Hacker News
SecurityWeek
Business Wire

entries.title: CamoLeak: GitHub Copilot Chat Exfiltrates Private Source Code via Hidden Markdown Instructions
entries.oneLineSummary: Researchers demonstrated that hidden markdown instructions in pull requests could make GitHub Copilot Chat leak private source code and secrets one character at a time through GitHub's Camo image proxy; GitHub fixed it before disclosure with no known in-the-wild use.

sources.title: Legit Security: CamoLeak Critical GitHub Copilot Vulnerability Leaks Private Source Code
Dark Reading: GitHub Copilot CamoLeak AI Attack Exfiltrates Data
The Register: GitHub Copilot Chat Vulnerability

entries.title: TennCare Connect / TEDS automated Medicaid terminations
entries.oneLineSummary: Tennessee's $400M+ automated Medicaid eligibility system wrongfully terminated or denied coverage for tens of thousands, and a federal court ruled the program violated the Medicaid Act, due process, and the ADA.

entries.title: Freysa autonomous AI agent tricked into releasing $47K crypto prize pool
entries.oneLineSummary: An autonomous on-chain AI agent hard-coded never to release its funds was prompt-tricked into transferring its entire ~13.19 ETH ($47,316) prize pool to a user.

sources.title: Human player outwits Freysa AI agent in $47,000 crypto challenge (The Block)

entries.title: eviCore "the dial" prior-authorization algorithm tuned to increase insurance denials
entries.date: October 2024 (investigation published; practices ongoing since at least 2021)
entries.oneLineSummary: eviCore, a Cigna-owned prior-authorization contractor serving about 100 million people, used an AI-backed algorithm insiders call "the dial" as the first screen on coverage requests and could tune it to route more requests to human reviewers to raise denial rates.

sources.title: ProPublica / Capitol Forum — "Not Medically Necessary": Inside the Company Helping America's Biggest Health Insurers Deny Coverage for Care
CNN — coverage of the ProPublica/Capitol Forum eviCore investigation

entries.title: Choice Home Warranty AI chatbot committed a $3,000 AC payout the company had not authorized
entries.oneLineSummary: Choice Home Warranty's customer-service chatbot agreed in writing to a $3,000 maximum cash payout toward an air conditioner replacement, a commitment the company initially disowned and honored only after a TV news investigation.

entries.oneLineSummary: A prompt-injected ChatGPT-powered dealer chatbot agreed in conversation to sell a 2024 Chevrolet Tahoe for $1 and called it legally binding; the exchange was text only and no sale occurred.

entries.title: Citigroup $444B Basket: Hard Blocks Caught ~$248B, No Notional Ceiling on the Rest
entries.oneLineSummary: A Citigroup trader clicked through a single pop-up listing 711 warning messages — only the first 18 visible without scrolling — and released a $444B basket order instead of the intended $58M; ~$1.4B sold before cancellation and the FCA/PRA fined Citigroup £61.6M.

entries.title: Cigna PxDx Batch Rubber-Stamp Denials
entries.oneLineSummary: Cigna's PxDx system was used to deny 300,000+ medical claims over about two months in 2022, with reviewing doctors averaging a reported 1.2 seconds per denial.

entries.title: Air Canada Held Liable for Chatbot's Misstated Bereavement Refund Policy
entries.oneLineSummary: Air Canada's website chatbot misstated the airline's bereavement fare policy as retroactively claimable, a customer booked on that basis, and a BC tribunal ordered the airline to pay $812.02 CAD for negligent misrepresentation.

entries.title: Zillow Offers iBuyer pricing algorithm drove $500M+ loss and wind-down
entries.oneLineSummary: Zillow's automated home-pricing algorithm made binding purchase offers above future resale values, forcing a $500M+ write-down and the shutdown of Zillow Offers.

entries.title: Dutch childcare benefits scandal (Toeslagenaffaire): nationality-based fraud-risk profiling wrongly accused ~26,000 families
entries.oneLineSummary: The Dutch Tax Administration used a fraud-risk classification method and nationality as a risk indicator to wrongly accuse about 26,000 families of childcare-benefit fraud, ordering many to repay large sums and causing severe hardship.

entries.title: Robert Williams wrongfully arrested after Detroit facial recognition false match
entries.oneLineSummary: A facial recognition system falsely matched Robert Williams to surveillance footage of a store theft, leading Detroit police to wrongfully arrest and detain him for about 30 hours.

entries.title: Michigan MiDAS system auto-adjudicated tens of thousands of false unemployment fraud determinations
entries.oneLineSummary: Michigan's MiDAS system automatically flagged unemployment claimants as fraudulent with little or no human review, falsely accusing tens of thousands and triggering wage garnishment and tax-refund seizures.

entries.title: Australia Robodebt: Unlawful Automated Welfare Debt Calculation
entries.oneLineSummary: Australia's Robodebt used an unlawful income-averaging method to automatically calculate and issue welfare debts, unlawfully raising approximately A$1.76 billion in debts against more than 433,000 people; the roughly A$751 million actually recovered was later repaid under a Federal Court settlement.

sources.title: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme Final Report

entries.title: Everbright Securities Arbitrage System Runaway Orders with Undisclosed Insider Hedge Cover Trade
entries.oneLineSummary: Everbright Securities' arbitrage system generated 23.4 billion yuan in erroneous buy orders with no enforcement ceiling, and the desk then executed a massive insider hedge before disclosing the error to the market.

entries.title: Knight Capital $440M Runaway Trading Loss
entries.oneLineSummary: Dormant algorithmic trading feature reactivated by configuration flag error, executing millions of unintended orders and causing about $440 million in losses within 45 minutes.

entries.title: 2010 Flash Crash triggered by automated sell algorithm
entries.oneLineSummary: An automated sell program dumped $4.1 billion of E-mini S&P 500 futures in about 20 minutes without regard to price or time, contributing to a roughly 998-point intraday drop in the Dow.
